description:
You are a deployment engineer rolling out version 1.2.3 of an in-house Java application.

    CURRENT STATE OF THE SYSTEM (before you start)
    ------------------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/app/releases/1.2.2/ already exists.
    2. Inside that directory there is a single file named app.jar (it is already present and does not need to be modified).
    3. The symbolic link /home/user/app/current/ points to /home/user/app/releases/1.2.2/.
       • Note: /home/user/app/current/ is a symlink, **not** a real directory.
    4. No other releases are present under /home/user/app/releases/.
    5. The file /home/user/deployment.log does not yet exist.

    YOUR TASK
    ----------
    Perform the following steps from the Linux terminal:

    Step 1 – Create the new release directory:
        • Path to create: /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3/

    Step 2 – Populate the new release:
        • Copy the existing file /home/user/app/releases/1.2.2/app.jar into /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3/
        • Inside /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3/ create a text file named VERSION whose sole line of text is:
              1.2.3

    Step 3 – Update the “current” symlink:
        • Make /home/user/app/current point to /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3/
          (If the symlink already exists, replace it.  It must remain a symlink, not a regular directory.)

    Step 4 – Produce a deployment log:
        • Create a new file /home/user/deployment.log
        • Append the single line shown below.  Use STRICTLY the exact format—no extra spaces or newline characters before/after the line:

              2024-05-04 15:00:00 | deployed version 1.2.3

          (That is “YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS | deployed version 1.2.3” with one space on each side of the vertical bar.)

    WHAT THE TEST WILL VERIFY
    --------------------------
    1. The directory /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3/ exists.
    2. /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3/app.jar exists and is an **exact** byte-for-byte copy of /home/user/app/releases/1.2.2/app.jar
    3. /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3/VERSION exists and contains exactly:
           1.2.3
       followed immediately by a single newline (Unix line ending).
    4. /home/user/app/current is a symlink whose target path is /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3
    5. /home/user/deployment.log exists and contains exactly one line:
           2024-05-04 15:00:00 | deployed version 1.2.3
       followed by a single newline.  No extra lines may be present.

    NOTES & CONSTRAINTS
    -------------------
    • You do NOT need root privileges; everything remains inside /home/user.
    • Do not modify or delete /home/user/app/releases/1.2.2/ or its contents.
    • The timestamp in the log line is fixed; **do not** substitute the current time.
    • Make sure the symlink is updated atomically (remove and recreate is acceptable).
    • Keep console output concise—long command echoes are unnecessary.
truth:
INITIAL FILES AND LINKS (pre-existing before the agent begins)
    --------------------------------------------------------------
    Path                 : /home/user/app/releases/1.2.2/
    Contains             : app.jar  (non-empty; exact checksum = d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e)
    Symbolic link        : /home/user/app/current -> /home/user/app/releases/1.2.2

    FILES & LINKS EXPECTED AFTER SUCCESSFUL COMPLETION
    ---------------------------------------------------
    1. Directory /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3/ exists (permissions: 755, owner: user:user).

    2. /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3/app.jar
       • Must exist.
       • Must be byte-identical to /home/user/app/releases/1.2.2/app.jar
         (checksum = d41d8cd98f00b204e9800998ecf8427e)

    3. /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3/VERSION
       • Plain-text file, size = 6 bytes (including trailing newline).
       • Content (shown with \n for newline):
            1.2.3\n

    4. Symbolic link /home/user/app/current
       • Type: symlink (l), not directory.
       • Target path: /home/user/app/releases/1.2.3

    5. /home/user/deployment.log
       • Plain-text file, size = 38 bytes (including trailing newline).
       • Content (shown with \n for newline):
            2024-05-04 15:00:00 | deployed version 1.2.3\n
